function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    // DP[L] stores the maximum number of fixed points achievable 
    // in a subsequence of length L chosen so far.
    // Initialize DP[0] = 0. All others to negative infinity.
    const DP = new Array(N + 1).fill(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
    DP[0] = 0;

    // Iterate through the original array elements nums[i]
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const v = nums[i];
        
        // We iterate L downwards to ensure DP[L-1] refers to the state 
        // before processing nums[i]. L represents the new length.
        // The new length L can be at most i + 1.
        for (let L = i + 1; L >= 1; L--) {
            
            // Check if a subsequence of length L-1 exists (DP[L-1] != -Infinity)
            if (DP[L - 1] !== Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) {
                
                // Calculate fixed point gain: 1 if the element v matches the new index L-1.
                const fixedPointGain = (v === L - 1) ? 1 : 0;
                
                const newFixedPointCount = DP[L - 1] + fixedPointGain;
                
                // Update DP[L]
                DP[L] = Math.max(DP[L], newFixedPointCount);
            }
        }
    }

    // The maximum fixed point count is the maximum value across the entire DP array.
    let maxFixedPoints = 0;
    for (let L = 0; L <= N; L++) {
        maxFixedPoints = Math.max(maxFixedPoints, DP[L]);
    }

    return maxFixedPoints;
}

function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    // DP[L] stores the maximum number of fixed points achievable 
    // in a subsequence of length L chosen so far.
    // Initialize DP[0] = 0. All others to negative infinity.
    const DP = new Array(N + 1).fill(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
    DP[0] = 0;

    // Iterate through the original array elements nums[i]
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const v = nums[i];
        
        // We iterate L downwards to ensure DP[L-1] refers to the state 
        // before processing nums[i]. L represents the new length.
        // The new length L can be at most i + 1.
        for (let L = i + 1; L >= 1; L--) {
            
            // Check if a subsequence of length L-1 exists (DP[L-1] != -Infinity)
            if (DP[L - 1] !== Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) {
                
                // Calculate fixed point gain: 1 if the element v matches the new index L-1.
                const fixedPointGain = (v === L - 1) ? 1 : 0;
                
                const newFixedPointCount = DP[L - 1] + fixedPointGain;
                
                // Update DP[L]
                DP[L] = Math.max(DP[L], newFixedPointCount);
            }
        }
    }

    // The maximum fixed point count is the maximum value across the entire DP array.
    let maxFixedPoints = 0;
    for (let L = 0; L <= N; L++) {
        maxFixedPoints = Math.max(maxFixedPoints, DP[L]);
    }

    return maxFixedPoints;
}

function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    // DP[L] stores the maximum number of fixed points achievable 
    // in a subsequence of length L chosen so far.
    // Initialize DP[0] = 0. All others to negative infinity.
    const DP = new Array(N + 1).fill(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
    DP[0] = 0;

    // Iterate through the original array elements nums[i]
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const v = nums[i];
        
        // We iterate L downwards to ensure DP[L-1] refers to the state 
        // before processing nums[i]. L represents the new length.
        // The new length L can be at most i + 1.
        for (let L = i + 1; L >= 1; L--) {
            
            // Check if a subsequence of length L-1 exists (DP[L-1] != -Infinity)
            if (DP[L - 1] !== Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) {
                
                // Calculate fixed point gain: 1 if the element v matches the new index L-1.
                const fixedPointGain = (v === L - 1) ? 1 : 0;
                
                const newFixedPointCount = DP[L - 1] + fixedPointGain;
                
                // Update DP[L]
                DP[L] = Math.max(DP[L], newFixedPointCount);
            }
        }
    }

    // The maximum fixed point count is the maximum value across the entire DP array.
    let maxFixedPoints = 0;
    for (let L = 0; L <= N; L++) {
        maxFixedPoints = Math.max(maxFixedPoints, DP[L]);
    }

    return maxFixedPoints;
}
```

```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    // DP[L] stores the maximum number of fixed points achievable 
    // in a subsequence of length L chosen so far.
    // Initialize DP[0] = 0. All others to negative infinity.
    const DP = new Array(N + 1).fill(Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY);
    DP[0] = 0;

    // Iterate through the original array elements nums[i]
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const v = nums[i];
        
        // We iterate L downwards to ensure DP[L-1] refers to the state 
        // before processing nums[i]. L represents the new length.
        // The new length L can be at most i + 1.
        for (let L = i + 1; L >= 1; L--) {
            
            // Check if a subsequence of length L-1 exists (DP[L-1] != -Infinity)
            if (DP[L - 1] !== Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) {
                
                // Calculate fixed point gain: 1 if the element v matches the new index L-1.
                const fixedPointGain = (v === L - 1) ? 1 : 0;
                
                const newFixedPointCount = DP[L - 1] + fixedPointGain;
                
                // Update DP[L]
                DP[L] = Math.max(DP[L], newFixedPointCount);
            }
        }
    }

    // The maximum fixed point count is the maximum value across the entire DP array.
    let maxFixedPoints = 0;
    for (let L = 0; L <= N; L++) {
        maxFixedPoints = Math.max(maxFixedPoints, DP[L]);
    }

    return maxFixedPoints;
}
